title: Spodiopogon pogonanthus
taxonomy: Spodiopogon pogonanthus is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Spodiopogon, which is native to Australia. The species Spodiopogon pogonanthus is further classified as a type of tussock grass, characterized by its dense growth habit. The Poaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, comprising many different species of grasses. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other grasses.

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
